# VETT — llms.txt

> Methodology-first synthetic-respondent research platform. AI personas
> calibrated to your audience answer your survey via Anthropic Claude;
> 13 supported research frameworks (Van Westendorp, MaxDiff, NPS,
> brand-health funnel, etc. — each peer-reviewed in the academic
> literature). VETT outputs are synthetic-respondent simulations of
> those frameworks. Treat as fast directional signal, not panel-grade
> truth.

## What VETT is

VETT runs market-research missions backed by synthetic respondents
generated by Anthropic Claude. The model produces a population of
personas matching your audience spec (geography, demographics,
behavioral, B2B filters), simulates each persona answering your
questions in their own voice, then synthesizes the responses into
methodology-specific deliverables.

What we explicitly do NOT do:
- Panel real consumers
- Collect or store PII from "respondents" (there are no real ones)
- Claim panel-level statistical validity for the outputs

What you should expect:
- Mission turnaround: 5-15 minutes typical (scales with respondent count)
- Output: executive summary + per-question aggregates + methodology
  cards + PDF / PPTX / XLSX / CSV / JSON exports
- Auto-refunds: partial delivery triggers a Stripe partial refund;
  full failures trigger a full refund within 24 hours

## Methodologies supported (13)

Each methodology has a dedicated input collector, simulation prompt,
synthesis pipeline, and results page.

1. validate — Concept Test (single-concept monadic acceptance)
2. compare — Sequential Monadic comparison with rotation
3. marketing — Ad Effectiveness (creative diagnostics + KPIs)
4. creative_attention — Frame-by-frame attention prediction +
   24-emotion taxonomy + platform fit + channel benchmarks
5. brand_lift — Incrementality (exposed vs control) across the
   9-category industry framework (aided/unaided recall, attribution,
   awareness, message association, favorability, purchase intent,
   recommendation intent, ad recall)
6. pricing — Van Westendorp + Gabor-Granger price elasticity
7. feature_roadmap — MaxDiff + Kano feature prioritization
8. customer_satisfaction — NPS + CSAT + CES with touchpoint anchoring
9. competitor — Brand Health Tracker with attribute battery + funnel
10. naming_messaging — Monadic + Paired comparison + TURF reach
11. churn_research — Driver Tree + Win-Back analysis
12. audience_profiling — K-means segmentation (in progress)
13. market_entry — Multi-market routing (in progress)

## Pricing

Three pricing ladders keyed to delivery shape:

Per-respondent missions (validate, naming_messaging, marketing,
pricing, feature_roadmap, customer_satisfaction, compare, competitor,
churn_research):
- Sniff Test     5 personas   $9
- Validate      10 personas   $35
- Confidence    50 personas   $99
- Deep Dive    250 personas   $299
- Scale       1000 personas   $899
- Enterprise  5000 personas  $1990

Brand Lift (statistical sample sizes, exposed + control split):
- Pulse        50 personas    $99
- Tracker     200 personas    $299
- Wave        500 personas    $599
- Enterprise 2000 personas  $1499

Creative Attention (flat per-asset, no respondent count):
- Image        1 image  $19
- Video        1 video  $39
- Bundle       5 assets $79
- Series      20 assets $249

Targeting surcharges apply for narrow multi-criteria targeting.
Extra questions beyond the included 10 are $1.50 each.

## How a mission runs

1. User describes the research question in plain language.
2. AI clarifies the brief (3 quick questions), builds a survey with
   appropriate question types, and suggests targeting.
3. AI generates personas matching the targeting + screener spec,
   simulates each persona's answers via Claude, and runs a
   methodology-specific synthesis pipeline.
4. User receives an executive summary, per-question distributions,
   methodology cards (price elasticity curves, attention decay,
   funnel charts, etc.), and exportable deliverables.

## Markets

VETT supports research in 160+ countries. Strong coverage in MENA
(UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Lebanon, Jordan), GCC, Europe (UK,
Germany, France, Spain), North America (US, Canada), India, Singapore,
Brazil, and frontier markets.
